description_long: This is a heavily weathered manual wheelchair with a distressed, patchwork metal frame and large spoked rear wheels. The frame is constructed from visibly welded metal panels, showing significant rust, scratches, and stains, giving it a rugged, industrial appearance. The seat and backrest are made from the same metal material, with no visible padding, and are attached to the frame with prominent rivets and seams. The wheelchair features two large rear wheels with metal spokes and two smaller front caster wheels, all showing signs of wear and corrosion. The push handles at the back are angular and robust, designed for manual operation, while the footrests are flat, rectangular metal plates extending forward. The overall design suggests durability and utilitarian function, but the condition indicates long-term exposure to harsh environments.
description: A rusted, patchwork metal wheelchair with large spoked wheels, angular push handles, and flat metal footrests. The frame and seat are heavily weathered, showing extensive wear and corrosion.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the patchwork metal backrest, angular push handles, and the tops of the large spoked wheels.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the large spoked rear wheel, flat metal footrests, and the welded, rusted frame structure.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the flat metal seat, footrests, and the open, boxy frame with visible wear and stains.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the large spoked rear wheel, angular push handles, and the distressed metal panels of the frame.
attribute_wheel_type: Large spoked metal rear wheels (2) and small solid front caster wheels (2), all showing rust and wear.
attribute_frame_design: Rigid, welded metal frame with patchwork panels and visible cross-bracing; boxy geometry with angular lines.
attribute_footrest_style: Fixed, flat rectangular metal footrests extending forward, non-detachable and non-elevating.
attribute_handlebar_configuration: Standard, straight metal push handles with angular ends, positioned at the top rear of the frame.
